task_description: Screen the following papers for relevance to: machine learning applications in biomedical research. Assign RELEVANT if the paper directly applies machine learning methods to a biomedical problem. Assign NOT_RELEVANT otherwise.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Protein Structure Prediction
papers.abstract: We present a transformer-based architecture for predicting protein tertiary structure from amino acid sequences. Trained on the Protein Data Bank, our model achieves state-of-the-art RMSD on CASP14 targets and generalises to novel fold families.
papers.methodology_hint: transformer neural network, supervised learning, RMSD evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: A transformer model for protein structure prediction that outperforms prior methods on CASP14 benchmarks.

papers.title: Optimal Transport and Multi-Marginal Welfare Economics
papers.abstract: We study multi-marginal optimal transport and derive new theoretical bounds on allocation efficiency in competitive welfare economics settings. Our proofs extend classical Kantorovich duality using functional-analytic techniques.
papers.methodology_hint: mathematical analysis, Kantorovich duality, theoretical proofs
papers.claimed_contribution: Extended Kantorovich duality for multi-marginal optimal transport with welfare economics interpretations.

papers.title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Drug Candidate Screening
papers.abstract: We apply convolutional neural networks to virtual screening of small-molecule drug candidates against a panel of oncology targets. Our model achieves a 3.2x improvement in hit rate over molecular docking baselines on a held-out test set of 50,000 compounds.
papers.topic_hint: machine learning, drug discovery, computational chemistry
papers.methodology_hint: convolutional neural networks, virtual screening, hit rate evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN-based virtual screening achieving 3.2x hit rate improvement over docking baselines across oncology targets.

papers.title: Graph Neural Networks for 30-Day Hospital Readmission Prediction
papers.abstract: Patient clinical histories are represented as heterogeneous graphs over diagnoses, medications, and procedures. A graph neural network trained on multi-hospital EHR data outperforms logistic regression and gradient boosting on 30-day readmission prediction, with AUROC 0.81 on a held-out cohort.
papers.topic_hint: machine learning, electronic health records, clinical prediction
papers.methodology_hint: graph neural networks, EHR modelling, AUROC evaluation
papers.claimed_contribution: GNN readmission predictor trained on EHR graphs, validated across multiple hospital sites.

papers.title: Nested Sampling for Gravitational Wave Parameter Estimation
papers.abstract: We develop a nested sampling framework for Bayesian parameter estimation of gravitational wave signals detected by LIGO-Virgo. The approach improves posterior coverage compared to MCMC for high-mass binary mergers and reduces wall-clock time by 40 percent.
papers.methodology_hint: nested sampling, Bayesian inference, MCMC comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Faster and more accurate posterior estimation for gravitational wave parameters using nested sampling.
